## Page 392

### [p392_c0001] DRAFT
### [p392_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p392_c0003] DEFICIENCIES
[p392_c0004] We identified, and included below, deficiencies involving the MTA Consolidated IT Department's internal control over financial reporting for the year ended December 31, 2020, that have not been previously communicated in writing or orally, by others within the MTA, or by us.
### [p392_c0005] 1. WCIS Oracle Database Password Parameters
### [p392_c0006] Agency:
[p392_c0007] New York City Transit Authority
### [p392_c0008] Criteria:
[p392_c0009] The identity of users should be authenticated to the systems software through passwords or other authentication mechanisms, in compliance with entity security policies. The use of passwords incorporates policies on periodic change, confidentiality, and password format (e.g., password length, alphanumeric content, expiration, account lockout).
### [p392_c0010] Condition:
[p392_c0011] D&T noted that password settings for the DEFAULT profile (minimum length, expiration, lockout, history, & complexity) and the ADMIN_PROFILE profile (minimum length, expiration, history, & complexity) on the Oracle database supporting the WCIS application are not set in compliance with the company. Given that both profiles have user accounts assigned to them, including individual database administrator accounts, these settings are inappropriate. There are individual user accounts assigned to the profile and therefore passwords for those individuals do not adhere to the policy for minimum length, expiration, lockout, history, & complexity.
### [p392_c0012] Cause:
[p392_c0013] Although Management has a formal policy as it pertains to password parameters, they have not sufficiently reinforced the policy and haven't monitored adherence to the policy. D&T noted that the root cause was attributed to oversight on behalf of management.
### [p392_c0014] Effect:
[p392_c0015] Security mechanisms are inadequate, ineffective, or inconsistent due to lack of established security policies and standards. This increases the risk of unauthorized access to the Oracle database supporting the WCIS application which could potentially lead to inappropriate changes to application data.
### [p392_c0016] Recommendation:
[p392_c0017] We recommend that MTA HQ management either align the password parameters for the DEFAULT and ADMIN_PROFILE profiles on the Oracle Database with MTA HQ security policies and industry best practices or restrict DEFAULT profile to system/generic accounts (i.e. removing the user accounts).
### [p392_c0018] Financial Statement Impact:
[p392_c0019] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by other controls and factors.
### [p392_c0020] Management Response (2020):
[p392_c0021] _- 6 -_
[p392_c0022] _Master Page # 392 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 393

### [p393_c0001] DRAFT
### [p393_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p393_c0003] 2. WCIS Oracle Database Privileged Access
### [p393_c0004] Agency:
[p393_c0005] New York City Transit Authority
### [p393_c0006] Criteria:
[p393_c0007] Privileged-level access (e.g., security administrators) is authorized and appropriately restricted.
### [p393_c0008] Condition:
[p393_c0009] During our audit procedures, D&T noted that five users retained inappropriate access to the Oracle database supporting WCIS. Through inquiry with management, we noted that these users no longer work at the MTA, but their database accounts remained active.
### [p393_c0010] Cause:
[p393_c0011] Although Management has a formal policy as it pertains to privileged access and the removal of access for terminated employees, they have not sufficiently reinforced the policy and haven't revoked accounts that no longer require elevated access.
### [p393_c0012] Effect:
[p393_c0013] Given that privileged access has not been appropriately restricted, there is a risk of unauthorized user access modifications that would circumvent the provisioning and deprovisioning processes.
### [p393_c0014] Recommendation:
[p393_c0015] We recommend that New York Transit Authority management appropriately restrict privileged access accounts when the access is no longer required.
### [p393_c0016] Financial Statement Impact:
[p393_c0017] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by other controls and factors.
### [p393_c0018] Management Response (2020):
[p393_c0019] _- 7 -_
[p393_c0020] _Master Page # 393 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 394

### [p394_c0001] DRAFT
### [p394_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p394_c0003] 3. Change Management - Access to Production – WCIS Application
### [p394_c0004] Agency:
[p394_c0005] New York City Transit Authority
### [p394_c0006] Criteria:
[p394_c0007] Access to implement changes into the application production environment should be appropriately restricted to the IT Security Administrator and segregated from application developers.
### [p394_c0008] Condition:
[p394_c0009] D&T noted that there is an insufficient segregation of duties as there are two developers with access to promote changes to the WCIS production environment. Additionally, D&T identified four accounts with inappropriate access to promote changes as they belong to terminated users.
[p394_c0010] As such, it is possible for a developer to migrate their own change into the WCIS production environment without appropriate change management testing and approvals.
### [p394_c0011] Cause:
[p394_c0012] MTA IT has not appropriately segregated logical access for developers; they are granted access to both development and production environments without sufficient monitoring controls.
### [p394_c0013] Effect:
[p394_c0014] Given that logical access has not been appropriately segregated, there is a risk of unauthorized changes being implemented into the production environment that would circumvent the change management process.
### [p394_c0015] Recommendation:
[p394_c0016] We recommend that MTA IT restrict logical access to the developers such that they cannot move changes from the test environment into production.
### [p394_c0017] Financial Statement Impact:
[p394_c0018] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by the following control:
- [p394_c0019] 1. As changes are required to be tested and approved prior to implementation in production as a part of the control, D&T noted that an effective control would mitigate the risk of inappropriate changes to the application or supporting database.
### [p394_c0020] Status Update (2020):
[p394_c0021] _- 8 -_
[p394_c0022] _Master Page # 394 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 395

### [p395_c0001] DRAFT
### [p395_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p395_c0003] 4. AFC Terminations
### [p395_c0004] Agency:
[p395_c0005] New York City Transit Authority
### [p395_c0006] Criteria:
[p395_c0007] Access for terminated and/or transferred users is removed or modified in a timely manner.
### [p395_c0008] Condition:
[p395_c0009] During our audit procedures, D&T noted that access to the AFC application was not disabled in a timely manner for six (6) out of twenty five (25) sampled terminated users.
### [p395_c0010] Cause:
[p395_c0011] The root cause of this deficiency is that IT Management did not adhere to the user deprovisioning process when removing access for the terminated users identified in this finding.
### [p395_c0012] Effect:
[p395_c0013] Given that these accounts remain enabled, there is a risk that the accounts could be compromised by other users and result in unauthorized access to the application, which may create improper segregation of duties.
### [p395_c0014] Recommendation:
[p395_c0015] We recommend that New York Transit Authority management reinforce the termination process with end users and system administrators to ensure that access changes are appropriately communicated and removed in a timely manner when the access is no longer required.
### [p395_c0016] Financial Statement Impact:
[p395_c0017] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by other controls and factors.
### [p395_c0018] Management Response (2020):
[p395_c0019] _- 9 -_
[p395_c0020] _Master Page # 395 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 396

### [p396_c0001] DRAFT
### [p396_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p396_c0003] 5. Windows AD Network Privileged Access
### [p396_c0004] Agency:
[p396_c0005] New York City Transit Authority
### [p396_c0006] Criteria:
[p396_c0007] Privileged-level access (e.g., security administrators) is authorized and appropriately restricted.
### [p396_c0008] Condition:
[p396_c0009] During our audit procedures, D&T noted that there are two (2) service accounts that are inappropriately granted privileged access to the Windows AD network supporting NYCT, as management is not aware of the purpose of the account and/or the users with access to each account.
### [p396_c0010] Cause:
[p396_c0011] D&T noted the root cause of this deficiency is that IT Management does not perform a periodic review of service accounts to recertify their business purpose and the appropriateness of access.
### [p396_c0012] Effect:
[p396_c0013] Given that privileged access has not been appropriately restricted, there is a risk of users with access to the noted service accounts have access privileges beyond those necessary to perform their assigned duties, which may create improper segregation of duties.
### [p396_c0014] Recommendation:
[p396_c0015] We recommend that management remove the inappropriate access and periodically recertify the appropriateness of service accounts.
### [p396_c0016] Financial Statement Impact:
[p396_c0017] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by other controls and factors.
### [p396_c0018] Management Response (2020):
[p396_c0019] _- 10 -_
[p396_c0020] _Master Page # 396 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 397

### [p397_c0001] DRAFT
### [p397_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p397_c0003] 6. TBTA Kronos Application Password Parameters
### [p397_c0004] Agency:
[p397_c0005] Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority ("TBTA")
### [p397_c0006] Criteria:
[p397_c0007] The identity of users should be authenticated to the systems software through passwords or other authentication mechanisms, in compliance with entity security policies. The use of passwords incorporates policies on periodic change, confidentiality, and password format (e.g., password length, alphanumeric content, expiration, account lockout).
### [p397_c0008] Condition:
[p397_c0009] - The password parameters for the 'Never Expire' logon profile in the TBTA Kronos application are not set in line with the company password policy for the following parameters: password minimum length, password expiration, lockout threshold, password history and password complexity.
[p397_c0010] - In order to promote changes into production, the members of the TBTA Kronos application team use the username and password for a single shared account to access the Kronos Workforce Integration Manager tool. The password to this shared account is not being stored in a secure location such as password vaulting.
### [p397_c0011] Cause:
[p397_c0012] Although Management has a formal policy as it pertains to password parameters, they have not sufficiently reinforced the policy and haven't monitored adherence to the policy. D&T noted that the root cause was attributed to oversight on behalf of management.
### [p397_c0013] Effect:
[p397_c0014] Security mechanisms are inadequate, ineffective, or inconsistent due to lack of established security policies and standards. This increases the risk of unauthorized access to the Kronos application which could potentially lead to inappropriate changes to the application or underlying data.
### [p397_c0015] Recommendation:
[p397_c0016] We recommend that MTA TBTA management update password parameters on the Kronos application network to align either to the MTA HQ security policies or industry best practices. We recommend that MTA TBTA management use a password vault or similar solution to secure the password for the shared account used to access the Kronos Workforce Integration Manager.
### [p397_c0017] Financial Statement Impact:
[p397_c0018] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by other controls and factors.
### [p397_c0019] Management Response (2020):
[p397_c0020] _- 11 -_
[p397_c0021] _Master Page # 397 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 398

### [p398_c0001] DRAFT
### [p398_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p398_c0003] 7. TBTA Kronos New Access Provisioning
### [p398_c0004] Agency:
[p398_c0005] Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority (“TBTA”)
### [p398_c0006] Criteria:
[p398_c0007] Management approves the nature and extent of user-access privileges for new and modified user access, including standard application profiles/roles, critical financial reporting transactions, and segregation of duties.
### [p398_c0008] Condition:
[p398_c0009] During testing D&T noted that the request for access for the new hire sample did not specify the level of access the user should be granted in the TBTA Kronos application. As such, D&T was unable to validate that the type of access granted in the application matches the type of access requested for the new hire.
### [p398_c0010] Cause:
[p398_c0011] The root cause of this deficiency is that IT Management did not adhere to the user provisioning process when requesting for new access for a user in the application.
### [p398_c0012] Effect:
[p398_c0013] Without appropriate documentation related to the access provisioning process, there is a risk that users will be granted access privileges beyond those necessary to perform their assigned duties, which may create improper segregation of duties.
### [p398_c0014] Recommendation:
[p398_c0015] We recommend that MTA TBTA management reinforce the access request process with end users and system administrators to ensure that access requests are appropriately documented.
### [p398_c0016] Financial Statement Impact:
[p398_c0017] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by other controls and factors.
### [p398_c0018] Management Response (2020):
[p398_c0019] _- 12 -_
[p398_c0020] _Master Page # 398 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 399

### [p399_c0001] DRAFT
### [p399_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p399_c0003] 8. TBTA Kronos and Oracle Database Terminations
### [p399_c0004] Agency:
[p399_c0005] Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority (“TBTA”)
### [p399_c0006] Criteria:
[p399_c0007] Access for terminated and/or transferred users is removed or modified in a timely manner.
### [p399_c0008] Condition:
[p399_c0009] During our audit procedures, D&T noted that there is not a process in place for the TBTA Kronos application team to be notified when users are terminated. As such, management is not disabling accounts belonging to terminated users in a timely manner.
### [p399_c0010] Cause:
[p399_c0011] The root cause of this deficiency is that IT Management does not have a formal user deprovisioning process in place to remove access for terminated users on the application or supporting database.
### [p399_c0012] Effect:
[p399_c0013] There is a risk that the accounts could be compromised and result in unauthorized access to the application, which may create improper segregation of duties.
### [p399_c0014] Recommendation:
[p399_c0015] We recommend that TBTA management design a process to remove access from the Kronos application and supporting Oracle database when users are terminated from TBTA.
### [p399_c0016] Financial Statement Impact:
[p399_c0017] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by other controls and factors.
### [p399_c0018] Management Response (2020):
[p399_c0019] _- 13 -_
[p399_c0020] _Master Page # 399 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 400

### [p400_c0001] DRAFT
### [p400_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p400_c0003] 9. Change Management - Access to Production – TBTA Kronos Application
### [p400_c0004] Agency:
[p400_c0005] Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority (“TBTA”)
### [p400_c0006] Criteria:
[p400_c0007] Access to implement changes into the application production environment should be appropriately restricted to the IT Security Administrator and segregated from application developers.
### [p400_c0008] Condition:
[p400_c0009] D&T noted the users with access to promote changes into production for the TBTA Kronos application are also the users who develop the changes. This is a segregation of duties risk. Management does not have a formal retrospective review in place where a full listing of changes promoted into production is exported and reviewed on an established frequency.
### [p400_c0010] Cause:
[p400_c0011] The root cause of this deficiency is that the team managing the relevant application and its database is small and IT management is not able to appropriately segregate the users who are developing changes and the users who are promoting changes into production. Additionally, IT management has not recognized and addressed this risk by formalizing a monitoring review over changes promoted into production for the relevant application and database.
### [p400_c0012] Effect:
[p400_c0013] Given that logical access has not been appropriately segregated, there is a risk of unauthorized changes being implemented into the production environment that would circumvent the change management process.
### [p400_c0014] Recommendation:
[p400_c0015] We recommend that MTA IT design and implement a review control to monitor changes promoted into the production environment.
### [p400_c0016] Financial Statement Impact:
[p400_c0017] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by other controls and factors.
### [p400_c0018] Management Response (2020):
[p400_c0019] _- 14 -_
[p400_c0020] _Master Page # 400 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 401

### [p401_c0001] DRAFT
### [p401_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p401_c0003] 10. ORT Application – User Access Recertification
### [p401_c0004] Agency:
[p401_c0005] Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority (“TBTA”)
### [p401_c0006] Criteria:
[p401_c0007] User account recertifications / entitlement reviews are performed by the IT Department and application owners.
### [p401_c0008] Condition:
[p401_c0009] D&T noted that for the ORT application, there is no periodic user account recertification / entitlement review performed by the IT Department or ORT application owners.
### [p401_c0010] Cause:
[p401_c0011] The root cause of this deficiency is that IT Management does not have a formal user access review process in place to recertify access for the users who have access to the relevant application.
### [p401_c0012] Effect:
[p401_c0013] Given that logical access is not periodically reviewed, there is a risk that users have access privileges beyond those necessary to perform their assigned duties, which may create improper segregation of duties.
### [p401_c0014] Recommendation:
[p401_c0015] We recommend that MTA IT design and implement a review control to periodically recertify the appropriateness of users with access to ORT and their related system privileges.
### [p401_c0016] Financial Statement Impact:
[p401_c0017] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by other controls and factors.
### [p401_c0018] Management Response (2020):
[p401_c0019] _- 15 -_
[p401_c0020] _Master Page # 401 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 402

### [p402_c0001] DRAFT
### [p402_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p402_c0003] 11. ManageEngine Tool Password Parameters
### [p402_c0004] Agency:
[p402_c0005] Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority (“TBTA”)
### [p402_c0006] Criteria:
[p402_c0007] The identity of users should be authenticated to the systems software through passwords or other authentication mechanisms, in compliance with entity security policies. The use of passwords incorporates policies on periodic change, confidentiality, and password format (e.g., password length, alphanumeric content, expiration, account lockout).
### [p402_c0008] Condition:
[p402_c0009] The ManageEngine Tool password parameters are not set in accordance with MTA policy for the following parameters: minimum password length, expiration, password history, and complexity. Weak password parameters could potentially result in users gaining unauthorized access the applications.
### [p402_c0010] Cause:
[p402_c0011] Although Management has a formal policy as it pertains to password parameters, they have not sufficiently reinforced the policy and haven't monitored adherence to the policy. D&T noted that the root cause was attributed to oversight on behalf of management.
### [p402_c0012] Effect:
[p402_c0013] Security mechanisms are inadequate, ineffective, or inconsistent due to lack of established security policies and standards. This increases the risk of unauthorized access to the ManageEngine tool which could potentially lead to inappropriate changes being implemented to the production environment (tool is used to implement ORT changes to production).
### [p402_c0014] Recommendation:
[p402_c0015] We recommend that MTA TBTA management update password parameters on the ManageEngine tool to align either to the MTA HQ security policies or industry best practices.
### [p402_c0016] Financial Statement Impact:
[p402_c0017] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by other controls and factors.
### [p402_c0018] Management Response (2020):
[p402_c0019] _- 16 -_
[p402_c0020] _Master Page # 402 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

### [p403_c0004] Agency:

### [p403_c0006] Criteria:
[p403_c0007] Access for terminated and/or transferred users is removed or modified in a timely manner.
### [p403_c0008] Condition:
[p403_c0009] During our audit procedures, D&T noted that access to the ORT application was not disabled in a timely manner for three (3) out of five (5) sampled terminated users.

[p403_c0011] The root cause of this deficiency is that IT Management did not adhere to the user deprovisioning process when removing access for the terminated users identified in this finding.
### [p403_c0012] Effect:

[p403_c0015] We recommend that TBTA management reinforce the termination process with end users and system administrators to ensure that access changes are appropriately communicated and removed in a timely manner when the access is no longer required.

## Page 404

### [p404_c0001] DRAFT
### [p404_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p404_c0003] 13. ORT Batch Job Monitoring
### [p404_c0004] Agency:
[p404_c0005] Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority (“TBTA”)
### [p404_c0006] Criteria:
[p404_c0007] Automated scheduling tools have been implemented for the completeness of the flow of processing and are monitored by the IT Department.
### [p404_c0008] Condition:
[p404_c0009] During our audit procedures, D&T noted that management does not maintain documentation related to batch job monitoring. As such, we were unable to validate that management performs appropriate monitoring procedures around the completion of the relevant batch jobs and resolution of job errors.
### [p404_c0010] Cause:
[p404_c0011] The root cause of this deficiency is that Management does not maintain documentation around the monitoring of batch jobs, specifically error resolution.
### [p404_c0012] Effect:
[p404_c0013] There is a risk that production systems, programs, and/or jobs result in inaccurate, incomplete, or unauthorized processing of data.
### [p404_c0014] Recommendation:
[p404_c0015] We recommend that TBTA management retain documentation related to monitoring of batch job status and the resolution of any noted errors.
### [p404_c0016] Financial Statement Impact:
[p404_c0017] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by other controls and factors.
### [p404_c0018] Management Response (2020):
[p404_c0019] _- 18 -_
[p404_c0020] _Master Page # 404 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 405

### [p405_c0001] DRAFT
### [p405_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p405_c0003] 14. CSS Application, MNR Kronos Application, & Oracle Database Password Parameters
### [p405_c0004] Agency:
[p405_c0005] Metro-North Railroad Commuter Railroad Company (“MNR”)
### [p405_c0006] Criteria:
[p405_c0007] The identity of users should be authenticated to the systems software through passwords or other authentication mechanisms, in compliance with entity security policies. The use of passwords incorporates policies on periodic change, confidentiality, and password format (e.g., password length, alphanumeric content, expiration, account lockout).
### [p405_c0008] Condition:
[p405_c0009] CSS Application: The CSS password parameters do not have a lockout threshold set, whereas the company policy requires a lockout threshold of at least 5 attempts. Additionally, the password history is only 3, while the company policy requires 24. As such, the password parameters are not set in accordance with MTA security policies/industry best practices. Weak password parameters could potentially result in users gaining unauthorized access the applications.
### [p405_c0010] MNR Kronos Application:
- [p405_c0011] 1. The password parameters for the &SUPER_USER_LOGON Logon Profile in the MNR Kronos application are not in line with the company password policy for the following parameters: password expiration, lockout threshold, password history and password complexity. 2. In order to promote changes into production, the members of the MNR Kronos application team use the username and password for a single shared account to access the Kronos Workforce Integration Manager tool. The password to this shared account is not being stored in a secure location such as password vaulting.
[p405_c0012] MNR Kronos Database: For the DEFAULT profile in the Oracle database supporting the MNR Kronos application, there is a user account assigned to this profile and the password parameters on this profile are not in line with the company password policy or industry standards for the following parameters: minimum password length, complexity, password history, password expiration and lockout attempts.
### [p405_c0013] Cause:
[p405_c0014] Although Management has a formal policy as it pertains to password parameters, they have not sufficiently reinforced the policy and haven't monitored adherence to the policy. D&T noted that the root cause was attributed to oversight on behalf of management.
### [p405_c0015] Effect:
[p405_c0016] Security mechanisms are inadequate, ineffective, or inconsistent due to lack of established security policies and standards. This increases the risk of unauthorized access to the CSS application, Kronos application, and Oracle database supporting the Kronos application, which could potentially lead to inappropriate changes to the application or underlying data.
[p405_c0017] _- 19 -_
[p405_c0018] _Master Page # 405 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 406

### [p406_c0001] DRAFT
### [p406_c0002] Recommendation:
[p406_c0003] We recommend that MNR management align the password parameters for the CSS application and Kronos application with the MNR security policies and industry best practices. We recommend that MNR management use a password vault or similar solution to secure the password for the shared account used to access the Kronos Workforce Integration Manager. We recommend that MNR management either align the password parameters for the DEFAULT profile on the Oracle database with MTA HQ security policies and industry best practices or restrict DEFAULT profile to system/generic accounts (i.e. removing the user accounts).
### [p406_c0004] Financial Statement Impact:
[p406_c0005] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by other controls and factors.
### [p406_c0006] Management Response (2020):
[p406_c0007] _- 20 -_
[p406_c0008] _Master Page # 406 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 407

### [p407_c0001] DRAFT
### [p407_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p407_c0003] 15. Change Management - Access to Production – CSS Application & MNR Kronos Application
### [p407_c0004] Agency:
[p407_c0005] Metro-North Railroad Commuter Railroad Company (“MNR”)
### [p407_c0006] Criteria:
[p407_c0007] Access to implement changes into the application production environment should be appropriately restricted to the IT Security Administrator and segregated from application developers.
### [p407_c0008] Condition:
[p407_c0009] CSS Application: Programmers, who are users that have access to develop changes to the CSS application, have access to promote changes into the CSS production environment.
[p407_c0010] MNR Kronos Application and Database: The users with access to promote changes into production for the MNR Kronos application are also the users who develop the changes. This is a segregation of duties risk. Management does not have a formal retrospective review in place where a full listing of changes promoted into production is exported and reviewed on an established frequency.
### [p407_c0011] Cause:
[p407_c0012] The root cause of this deficiency is that the team managing the relevant application and its database is small and IT management is not able to appropriately segregate the users who are developing changes and the users who are promoting changes into production. Additionally, IT management has not recognized and addressed this risk by formalizing a monitoring review over changes promoted into production for the relevant application and database.
### [p407_c0013] Effect:
[p407_c0014] Given that logical access has not been appropriately segregated, there is a risk of unauthorized changes being implemented into the production environment that would circumvent the change management process.
### [p407_c0015] Recommendation:
[p407_c0016] We recommend that MTA IT design and implement a review control to monitor changes promoted into the production environment for both the CSS and Kronos applications.
### [p407_c0017] Financial Statement Impact:
[p407_c0018] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by other controls and factors.
### [p407_c0019] Management Response (2020):
[p407_c0020] _- 21 -_
[p407_c0021] _Master Page # 407 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 408

### [p408_c0001] DRAFT
### [p408_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p408_c0003] 16. MNR Kronos Application and Oracle Database Terminations
### [p408_c0004] Agency:
[p408_c0005] Metro-North Railroad Commuter Railroad Company (“MNR”)
### [p408_c0006] Criteria:
[p408_c0007] Access for terminated and/or transferred users is removed or modified in a timely manner.
### [p408_c0008] Condition:
[p408_c0009] During our audit procedures, D&T noted that there is not a process in place for the MNR Kronos application team to be notified when users are terminated. As such, management is not disabling accounts belonging to terminated users in a timely manner.
### [p408_c0010] Cause:
[p408_c0011] The root cause of this deficiency is that IT Management does not have a formal user deprovisioning process in place to remove access for terminated users on the application or supporting database.
### [p408_c0012] Effect:
[p408_c0013] There is a risk that the accounts could be compromised and result in unauthorized access to the application, which may create improper segregation of duties.
### [p408_c0014] Recommendation:
[p408_c0015] We recommend that MNR management design a process to remove access from the Kronos application and supporting Oracle database when users are terminated from MNR.
### [p408_c0016] Financial Statement Impact:
[p408_c0017] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by other controls and factors.
### [p408_c0018] Management Response (2020):
[p408_c0019] _- 22 -_
[p408_c0020] _Master Page # 408 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 409

### [p409_c0001] DRAFT
### [p409_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p409_c0003] 17. CAMS Oracle Database, TSS Oracle Database, & LIRR Kronos Application Password Parameters
### [p409_c0004] Agency:
[p409_c0005] Long Island Railroad Company (“LIRR”)
### [p409_c0006] Criteria:
[p409_c0007] The identity of users should be authenticated to the systems software through passwords or other authentication mechanisms, in compliance with entity security policies. The use of passwords incorporates policies on periodic change, confidentiality, and password format (e.g., password length, alphanumeric content, expiration, account lockout).
### [p409_c0008] Condition:
[p409_c0009] CAMS-FS Database: Password settings for the DEFAULT profile (minimum length, expiration, lockout, history, & complexity) and the FNC_APP_PROFILE profile (expiration, lockout, & history) are not set in compliance with the company. Given that both profiles have user accounts assigned to them, including individual database administrator accounts, these settings are inappropriate.
[p409_c0010] TSS Database: Password settings for the DEFAULT profile (minimum length, expiration, lockout, history, & complexity) and the FNC_APP_PROFILE profile (expiration, lockout, & history) are not set in compliance with the company. Given that both profiles have user accounts assigned to them, including individual database administrator accounts, these settings are inappropriate.
### [p409_c0011] LIRR Kronos Application:
- [p409_c0012] 1. The password parameters for the &SUPER_USER_LOGON Logon Profile in the LIRR Kronos application are not in line with the company password policy for the following parameters: password minimum length, password expiration, lockout threshold, password history and password complexity. 2. In order to promote changes into production, the members of the LIRR Kronos application team use the username and password for a single shared account to access the Kronos Workforce Integration Manager tool. The password to this shared account is not being stored in a secure location such as password vaulting.
### [p409_c0013] Cause:
[p409_c0014] Although Management has a formal policy as it pertains to password parameters, they have not sufficiently reinforced the policy and haven't monitored adherence to the policy. D&T noted that the root cause was attributed to oversight on behalf of management.
### [p409_c0015] Effect:
[p409_c0016] Security mechanisms are inadequate, ineffective, or inconsistent due to lack of established security policies and standards. This increases the risk of unauthorized access to the Oracle database supporting the CAMS application, Oracle database supporting the TSS application, and the Kronos application, which could potentially lead to inappropriate changes to the application or underlying data.
### [p409_c0017] Recommendation:
[p409_c0018] We recommend that LIRR management align the password parameters for the Kronos application with the LIRR security policies and industry best practices. We recommend that LIRR management use a password vault or similar solution to secure the password for the shared account used to access the Kronos Workforce
[p409_c0019] _- 23 -_
[p409_c0020] _Master Page # 409 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 411

### [p411_c0001] DRAFT
### [p411_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT CURRENT YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2020
### [p411_c0003] 18. TSS Application Privileged Access
### [p411_c0004] Agency:
[p411_c0005] Long Island Railroad Company (“LIRR”)
### [p411_c0006] Criteria:
[p411_c0007] Privileged-level access (e.g., security administrators) is authorized and appropriately restricted.
### [p411_c0008] Condition:
[p411_c0009] During our audit procedures, D&T noted that there is one user account with inappropriate privileged access on the TSS application per their job function and inquiry with management.
### [p411_c0010] Cause:
[p411_c0011] D&T noted the root cause of this deficiency is that IT Management does not perform a periodic review of administrator accounts to recertify the appropriateness of access.
### [p411_c0012] Effect:
[p411_c0013] Given that privileged access has not been appropriately restricted, there is a risk of users having access privileges beyond those necessary to perform their assigned duties, which may create improper segregation of duties.
### [p411_c0014] Recommendation:
[p411_c0015] We recommend that management remove the inappropriate access and periodically recertify the appropriateness of administrator accounts.
### [p411_c0016] Financial Statement Impact:
[p411_c0017] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by other controls and factors.
### [p411_c0018] Management Response (2020):
[p411_c0019] _- 25 -_
[p411_c0020] _Master Page # 411 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

### [p414_c0001] DRAFT
### [p414_c0002] MTA CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEPARTMENT PRIOR YEAR COMMENTS- DEFICIENCIES- 2019
[p414_c0003] We identified the following other deficiencies involving the Authority's internal control over financial reporting for the periods prior to the year ended December 31, 2020 that we wish to bring to your attention at this time.
### [p414_c0004] 21. Impact Oracle Database Password Parameters
### [p414_c0005] Agency:
[p414_c0006] MTAHQ
### [p414_c0007] Criteria:
[p414_c0008] The identity of users should be authenticated to the systems software through passwords or other authentication mechanisms, in compliance with entity security policies. The use of passwords incorporates policies on periodic change, confidentiality, and password format (e.g., password length, alphanumeric content, expiration, account lockout).
### [p414_c0009] Condition:
[p414_c0010] D&T noted that password settings for the DEFAULT profile on the Oracle database supporting the Impact application are not in line with the MTA Corporate Policy and/or industry standards. There are individual user accounts assigned to the profile and therefore passwords for those individuals do not adhere to the policy for minimum length, expiration, lockout, history, & complexity.
### [p414_c0011] Cause:
[p414_c0012] Although Management has a formal policy as it pertains to password parameters, they have not sufficiently reinforced the policy and haven't monitored adherence to the policy. D&T noted that the root cause was attributed to oversight on behalf of management.
### [p414_c0013] Effect:
[p414_c0014] Security mechanisms are inadequate, ineffective, or inconsistent due to lack of established security policies and standards. This increases the risk of unauthorized access to the Oracle database supporting the Impact application which could potentially lead to inappropriate changes to application data.
### [p414_c0015] Recommendation:
[p414_c0016] We recommend that MTA HQ management either align the password parameters for the DEFAULT profile on the Oracle Database with MTA HQ security policies and industry best practices or restrict DEFAULT profile to system/generic accounts (i.e. removing the user accounts).
### [p414_c0017] Financial Statement Impact:
[p414_c0018] No Impact – risk associated with this deficiency mitigated by other controls and factors.
### [p414_c0019] Management Response (2019):
[p414_c0020] Estimated Completion 3 rd qtr. 2020
[p414_c0021] Management concurs: domain password policies for HQ Domain has been remediated.

## Page 416

### [p416_c0001] DRAFT
### [p416_c0002] APPENDIX B
### [p416_c0003] DEFINITION
[p416_c0004] The definition of a deficiency is as follows:
[p416_c0005] A deficiency in internal control over financial reporting exists when the design or operation of a control does not allow management or employees, in the normal course of performing their assigned functions, to prevent, or detect and correct, misstatements on a timely basis. A deficiency in design exists when (a) a control necessary to meet the control objective is missing, or (b) an existing control is not properly designed so that, even if the control operates as designed, the control objective would not be met. A deficiency in operation exists when a properly designed control does not operate as designed or when the person performing the control does not possess the necessary authority or competence to perform the control effectively.
[p416_c0006] _- 30 -_
[p416_c0007] _Master Page # 416 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 417

### [p417_c0001] DRAFT
### [p417_c0002] APPENDIX C
### [p417_c0003] MANAGEMENT'S RESPONSIBILITY FOR, AND THE OBJECTIVES AND INHERENT LIMITATIONS OF, INTERNAL CONTROL OVER FINANCIAL REPORTING
[p417_c0004] The following comments concerning management's responsibility for internal control over financial reporting and the objectives and inherent limitations of internal control over financial reporting are included in generally accepted auditing standards.
### [p417_c0005] Management's Responsibility
[p417_c0006] The Company's management is responsible for the overall accuracy of the financial statements and their conformity with accounting principles generally accepted in the United States of America. In this regard, the Company's management is also responsible for designing, implementing and maintaining effective internal control over financial reporting.
### [p417_c0007] Objectives of Internal Control over Financial Reporting
[p417_c0008] An entity's internal control over financial reporting is a process effected by those charged with governance, management, and other personnel, designed to provide reasonable assurance regarding the preparation of reliable financial statements in accordance with accounting principles generally accepted in the United States of America. An entity's internal control over financial reporting includes those policies and procedures that (1) pertain to the maintenance of records that, in reasonable detail, accurately and fairly reflect the transactions and dispositions of the assets of the entity; (2) provide reasonable assurance that transactions are recorded as necessary to permit preparation of financial statements in accordance with accounting principles generally accepted in the United States of America, and that receipts and expenditures of the entity are being made only in accordance with authorizations of management and those charged with governance; and (3) provide reasonable assurance regarding prevention, or timely detection and correction, of unauthorized acquisition, use, or disposition of the entity's assets that could have a material effect on the financial statements.
### [p417_c0009] Inherent Limitations of Internal Control over Financial Reporting
[p417_c0010] Because of its inherent limitations, internal control over financial reporting may not prevent, or detect and correct, misstatements. Also, projections of any assessment of effectiveness to future periods are subject to the risk that controls may become inadequate because of changes in conditions, or that the degree of compliance with the policies or procedures may deteriorate.
[p417_c0011] * * * * *
[p417_c0012] _- 31 -_
[p417_c0013] _Master Page # 417 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 418

### [p418_c0001] DRAFT Deloitte.
### [p418_c0002] Deloitte & Touche LLP 30 Rockefeller Plaza New York, NY 10112 USA Tel: +1 212 436 2000 www.deloitte.com
### [p418_c0003] INDEPENDENT ACCOUNTANTS' REPORT ON APPLYING AGREED-UPON PROCEDURES
[p418_c0004] The Audit Committee of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority
[p418_c0005] We have performed the procedures enumerated below, which were agreed to by the Metropolitan Transportation Authority ("MTA"), the MTA Office of the Inspector General (the "IG") and the Auditor General of MTA, related to budget accountability for the IG for the year ended December 31, 2020. The IG is responsible for its budget accountability. The sufficiency of these procedures is solely the responsibility of the parties specified in this report. Consequently, we make no representation regarding the sufficiency of the procedures enumerated below either for the purpose for which this report has been requested or for any other purpose.
[p418_c0006] The procedures and the associated findings are as follows:
- [p418_c0007] 1. We utilized a random number generator to randomly select five employees from the MTA Headquarters - IG payroll register for December 2020 and we compared each individual's salary to an approved Personnel Action Form.
[p418_c0008] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p418_c0009] 2. We utilized a random number generator to randomly select two terminated employees from the listing of terminated employees for the year ended December 31, 2020 and we read each individual's Personnel Action Form. We read the payroll register subsequent to each employee's termination, and noted that these employees were removed from the payroll register.
[p418_c0010] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p418_c0011] 3. We compared the IG's total payroll per the payroll register for the month of December 2020 to the MTA's monthly expense report for the month of December 2020 and noted they agreed.
[p418_c0012] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p418_c0013] 4. We utilized a random number generator to randomly select five purchases made during the year from the general ledger for the year ended December 31, 2020, and noted that the related requisitions and purchase orders contained an approver's signature, and, where applicable, the bids were obtained.
[p418_c0014] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p418_c0015] 5. We utilized a random number generator to randomly select fifteen disbursements from the IG's General Fund for the year ended December 31, 2020 and compared the selected disbursements to
[p418_c0016] _Master Page # 418 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 419

[p419_c0001] supporting documentation and noted that the disbursements were authorized and supported by proper documentation.
[p419_c0002] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p419_c0003] 6. We utilized a random number generator to randomly select five petty cash vouchers for the last quarter ended December 31, 2020, and noted the vouchers were approved.
[p419_c0004] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p419_c0005] 7. We obtained the IG General Fund's bank reconciliation for the month of December 2020, and noted that the bank balance per the IG's General Fund's bank reconciliation agreed to the Authority's general ledger.
[p419_c0006] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p419_c0007] 8. We utilized a random number generator to randomly select five employee reimbursements from listing of employee reimbursements for the year ended December 31, 2020 and noted the reimbursements were supported by explanations, invoices, reimbursement applications, refund requisitions, purchase orders, and payment details. We utilized a random number generator to randomly select five employee expense reports from the listing of employee expenses reports for the year ended December 31, 2020, and noted the expense reports were supported by explanations, invoices, expense reports, purchase orders, and payment details.
[p419_c0008] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p419_c0009] 9. We obtained the only fixed asset addition made during the year ending December 31, 2020 and noted the purchase was approved and agreed the amount to the invoice and payment.
[p419_c0010] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p419_c0011] 10. Management informed us that there were no fixed asset disposals made during the year ended December 31, 2020 for the IG.
- [p419_c0012] 11. Management informed us that there was no depreciation expense during the year ended December 31, 2020 for the IG.
- [p419_c0013] 12. We compared the depreciation expense for the month of December 2020 to the MTA's general ledger and noted they were both $0.
[p419_c0014] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p419_c0015] 13. We read and compared the IG's monthly expense report to the MTA's monthly expense report for the month of December 2020.
[p419_c0016] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p419_c0017] 14. We compared the IG's 2020 expense budget to the actual expenditures for 2020, and noted total actual expenditures were less than the IG's 2020 expense budget by $ 4,446,086.
[p419_c0018] _- 2 -_

The agreed-upon procedures do not constitute an audit of financial statements or any part thereof, the objective of which is the expression of an opinion on the financial statements or a part thereof. Accordingly, we do not express such an opinion. Had we performed additional procedures, other matters might have come to our attention that would have been reported to you.

## Page 422

> [p422_c0002] The MTA logo, consisting of the letters 'MTA' in a bold, sans-serif font, is centered within a blue circle. The logo is set against a black background that spans the width of the page.
### [p422_c0003] Assessments Summary Snapshot
[p422_c0004] We are preparing to launch the Vulnerability (Risk) Assessment and Control Testing for 2021.
### [p422_c0005] Control Self Assessment Remediation Plan Summary
[p422_c0006] In January 2021 we reported 57 open remediation plans of which 5 were mitigating significant deficiencies.
[p422_c0007] We have closed 32 or 56% of the remediation plans.
[p422_c0008] All Agencies and departments are working on closing open remediation plans related to deficiencies identified during control testing
### [p422_c0009] Process Summary
[p422_c0010] Currently a significant number of business processes, risks and controls (PRCs) are being added to RSA Archer GRC

### [p426_c0003] Ethics and Compliance Program
[p426_c0004] Report to the Audit Committee July 2021
> [p426_c0005] The MTA logo, consisting of the letters "MTA" in white inside a grey circle, is located in the bottom left corner of the slide.
[p426_c0006] _Master Page # 426 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 427

### [p427_c0001] Master Page # 427 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021
> [p427_c0002] The MTA logo, consisting of the letters 'MTA' in white inside a blue circle.
### [p427_c0003] Objectives
- [p427_c0004] ❖ To develop, maintain, and/or revise the MTA Code of Ethics and ❖ To help develop, maintain, and/or revise all MTAHQ and agency policies and procedures. ❖ To initiate educational programs that enable MTA employees to understand the importance of compliance issues. ❖ To act as a channel of communication to receive compliance-related inquires and direct those inquires to appropriate resources within the MTA or other oversight agencies for investigation and resolution. ❖ To act as a final internal resource with which concerned parties may communicate after other formal channels and resources have been exhausted. ❖ To respond to alleged violations of rules, regulations, policies, procedures and Code of Ethics by evaluating or recommending the initiation of investigative procedures.
> [p427_c0005] The MTA logo, consisting of the letters 'MTA' in white inside a grey circle.

## Page 428

### [p428_c0001] Master Page # 428 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021
> [p428_c0002] The MTA logo, consisting of the letters 'MTA' in white inside a blue circle.
### [p428_c0003] Objectives
- [p428_c0004] ❖ To develop and oversee an Authority system for uniform handling of ethics violations. To act as an independent review and evaluation body to ensure that compliance issues and concerns within the MTA are appropriately evaluated, investigated and resolved. ❖ To identify potential areas of compliance vulnerability and risk via Internal Controls and provide general guidance on how to avoid or handle similar situations in the future. ❖ To ensure proper reporting of violations or potential violations to duly authorized enforcement agencies as appropriate and/or required. ❖ To establish an Ethics Helpline and provide for its direction and management. ❖ To institute and maintain an effective compliance communication program for the MTA, including promoting (a) use of the Ethics Helpline; (b) heightened awareness of the Code of Ethics, and (c) understanding of new and existing compliance issues and related policies and procedures. ❖ To develop an effective ethics and compliance training program , including appropriate introductory training for new employees as well as ongoing training for all employees and managers.
> [p428_c0005] The MTA logo, consisting of the letters 'MTA' in white inside a grey circle.

## Page 438

### [p438_c0001] Memorandum
> [p438_c0002] The logo of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) is a blue circle with the letters "MTA" in white.
### [p438_c0003] Metropolitan Transportation Authority
[p438_c0004] State of New York
[p438_c0005] Date July 21, 2021
[p438_c0006] To Audit Committee
[p438_c0007] From Lamond W. Kearse, Chief Ethics, Risk & Compliance Officer
[p438_c0008] Re Annual Report on 2020 Financial Interest Reporting Compliance
[p438_c0009] At its January 1992 meeting, this Committee requested an annual report regarding compliance by MTA Headquarters and its Agencies ("MTA") with the financial interest reporting requirements established by Public Officers Law and the MTA All Agency Code of Ethics.
[p438_c0010] Pursuant to MTA's Code of Ethics and Public Officers Law §73-a, each year MTA identifies those employees who are required to file a Financial Disclosure Statement based upon earning in excess of the statutory amount and those employees who are designated policy makers. This information is sent to Joint Commission on Public Ethics ("Commission"). Employees in these categories receive notices from the Commission that they are required to complete a Financial Disclosure Statement which must be returned to the Commission by the statutory deadline of May 15.
[p438_c0011] At its January 1996 board meeting, the Board authorized and directed the Chairman or his designees to actively assist the Commission in its enforcement of the State's financial disclosure reporting requirements and to impose disciplinary action in appropriate cases.
[p438_c0012] In accordance with the Board's directive, and in order to reinforce in the minds of all MTA employees the MTA's commitment to compliance with the State's financial disclosure requirements, the annexed memoranda regarding compliance with financial disclosure was sent to all MTA employees and Board Members concurrently with the notice from the Commission.
[p438_c0013] The MTA will also continue actively to assist the Commission in its enforcement of the law. As of the date of this memorandum, there are no delinquent filers from 2020.
[p438_c0014] This year the MTA has over 7,000 filers. MTA Corporate Compliance will be monitoring compliance closely in the coming year.
[p438_c0015] c: Patrick J. Foye Thomas Quigley Agency Ethics Officers
[p438_c0016] _Master Page # 438 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

## Page 439

### [p439_c0001] Memorandum
> [p439_c0002] The logo of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) is a blue circle with the letters "MTA" in white.
### [p439_c0003] Metropolitan Transportation Authority
[p439_c0004] State of New York
[p439_c0005] Date: April 5, 2021
[p439_c0006] To: All MTA Financial Disclosure Statement Filers
[p439_c0007] From: Lamond W. Kearse, Chief Compliance Officer
[p439_c0008] Re: Financial Disclosure Statement – 2021 Covering Calendar Year 2020
[p439_c0009] The New York State Joint Commission on Public Ethics (“JCOPE”) emailed notices regarding the New York State Annual Financial Disclosure Statement for 2021, covering calendar year 2020, to applicable employees. All employees who have been designated FDS filers have an obligation to file a Financial Disclosure Statement with JCOPE. This statement, whether completed on-line or hard copy, should be filled out carefully and thoughtfully and filed with JCOPE no later than Monday, May 17, 2021 pursuant to JCOPE’s instructions.
[p439_c0010] I urge all employees to review our MTA All Agency Code of Ethics. A copy of the code can be found on MTA’s Intranet, Internet or obtained from MTA Corporate Compliance. As you complete your financial disclosure form, you should be aware of any or actual potential conflicts of interest that you may need to report.
[p439_c0011] There is no exemption from filing for anyone who is away from work for any part of the calendar year or is working a reduced work schedule. New employees that are required to file, who commence service after the MTA submits its written list of financial filers, are required to file within 30 days of notification from JCOPE.
[p439_c0012] As you know, JCOPE is authorized to enforce the State’s ethics and financial reporting laws, which include the ability to impose a civil penalty up to $40,000 or to seek prosecution as Class A misdemeanor violations of those laws. Pursuant to Board guidelines, in addition to providing its full cooperation and assistance to JCOPE in its enforcement of the law, the MTA will impose such disciplinary action as may be appropriate in the case of violations.
[p439_c0013] Again, this year JCOPE may assess penalties for filings received after the statutory deadline. To avoid late fines and possible disciplinary action, your statement must be received by JCOPE no later than May 17, 2021 .
[p439_c0014] Should you find yourself in a situation that raises any question as to your obligations concerning conflicts of interest or whether you are required to file a financial disclosure
[p439_c0015] _Master Page # 439 of 457 - Audit Committee Meeting 7/19/2021_

[p440_c0001] statement with JCOPE, I encourage you to contact the Ethics Helpline at 888 U-ASK-MTA (888-827-5682) for guidance.

> [p455_c0002] The MTA logo, consisting of the letters 'MTA' in white inside a blue circle, is centered on a black horizontal bar.
### [p455_c0003] Executive Summary
### [p455_c0004] Period Snapshot
[p455_c0005] Remediation plans related to finding from Audit Services and control testing are being tracked and managed in RSA Archer. The Archer System providing increased transparency and accountability
[p455_c0006] July 2021 a total of 150 audit open remediation plans are under review
[p455_c0007] Year to Date (Jan-July 2021) staff have reviewed and closed 175 or 9.4% increase from prior reported (Jan-May) open remediation plans
[p455_c0008] In 2020 staff reviewed and closed 352 open audit remediation plans
[p455_c0009] _MTA Corporate Compliance_
